other_observers: none; I was not the original finder of this bird. Its presence had previously been reported on cobirds.
description: It was in with peeps, but much larger than any of them. It was similar in size to a Killdeer, but much chunkier, shorter, with a horizontal posture.  The underparts were clean white, at least as seen from behind.  The upperparts were gray. The wings had a scaly pattern. The back and mantle were a smooth gray.  The face was gray.  The bill was of medium length, quite thick near its base, then thinning, with a slight curve down.  The legs were paleBehaviors: feeding with peeps on a mudflatCall: none heardPlumage: juvenile
